description.para: These macros are used to set/get the clock mode in CSI2 Tx.
CCM - continuous clock mode. NCCM - non-continuous clock mode.
These handlers are used to determine the type of the interrupt handler being registered with the MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem.
Since the subsystem is tightly coupled with the CSI2 Tx Controller driver, the handlers from the sub core are promoted to the subsystem level so that the application can use them.

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Less no of lines recvd.
More no of lines recvd.
This typedef defines the different errors codes for Line Count status for a Virtual Channel when Frame End Generation is enabled.
Callback type which acts as a wrapper on top of CSI Callback.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XCSI2TXSS_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsi2txss_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XCsi2TxSs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each instance of the device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Index number of config table using BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the instance structure
address of the instance

simplesect.para: Index number of the config table

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the MIPI CSI subsystem and included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the subsystem. Initialization includes setting up the instance data for top level as well as all included sub-core therein, and ensuring the hardware is in a known stable state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
points to the configuration structure associated with the subsystem instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to configure the CSI lanes and interrupts that are to be handled by the application.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to XCsi2Tx.h for interrupt masks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is no of active lanes to be configure. This value ranges between 1 and 4.
Indicates Mask for enable interrupts.

listitem.para:
XST_SUCCESS on successful configuration of parameters
XST_FAILURE otherwise

simplesect.para: When EnableActiveLanes is 0, then the ActiveLanes parameter passed should be equal to Max Lanes of design.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to activate the CSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it activates the DPHY and CSI.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is used to denote whether to enable or disable the subsystem

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the CSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it resets the DPHY and CSI

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if all the sub core IP resets occur correctly
XST_FAILURE if reset fails for any sub core IP fails

briefdescription.para: This function reports list of cores included.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the short packets.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is to set the Line Synchronization packet Generation status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2 TX SS Instance to be worked on.
0 : DISABLE 1 : ENABLE

briefdescription.para: This function is to set Generic Short Packet Entries.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2 TX SS Instance to be worked on.
GSP 32 bit Entry

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the Pixel Mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0x0 - Single Pixel Mode 0x1 - Dual Pixel Mode 0x3 - Quad Pixel Mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the number of lanes configured in the IP.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Max number of lanes available in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the CSI2 Tx Subsystem Clock Mode as either Continuous (0) or Non-Continuous (1) mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
for Continuous Mode (0) or Non-continuous Mode (1)

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the CSI2 Tx Subsystem Clock Mode as either Continuous (0) or Non-Continuous (1) mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0 - Continuous Clock Mode 1 - Non-continuous Clock Mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if lanes are in ulps mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set lanes in ulps mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: This is to be called before starting the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is valid line count for the Virtual channel.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.
XST_FAILURE in case the core is already running.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is pointer to variable to be filled with line count for the Virtual channel

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.

briefdescription.para: This function performs self test on MIPI CSI Tx Subsystem sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCsi2TxSs_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to disable the interrupts in the CSI core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
Indicates Mask for enable interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ------------------------- ------------------------------- (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_WRG_LANE) IncorrectLaneCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_GSPFIFO_FULL) GSPFIFOCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_ULPS) DPhyUlpsCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LINEBUF_FULL) LineBufferCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_WRG_DATATYPE) WrgDataTypeCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_UNDERRUN_PIXEL) UnderrunPixelCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC0 LineCountErrVC0 (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC1 LineCountErrVC1 (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC2 LineCountErrVC2 (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC3 LineCountErrVC3

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallBackFunc

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.
